name: Crittenden Estate Villas
signature: Self-contained vineyard villas on the Crittenden lake — wake up, walk to the cellar door, and eat dinner at the adjoining Stillwater restaurant
editorNote:
Crittenden's lakeside villas are the most unfussed winery stay on the Peninsula and arguably the most complete one-location weekend. Each villa is self-contained — kitchen, fireplace, private terrace on the lake — and the layout of the estate means the cellar door, the Stillwater restaurant, and the tasting rooms are all a two-minute walk from your front door.

The villas are more lodge than luxury hotel, which is the right call for what this stay is actually for: a couple of nights in the middle of a vineyard, breakfast on the terrace, a tasting before lunch, the restaurant at night, and no reason to get in the car until the morning you drive home. The private-feeling grounds make it feel like you have the whole estate to yourself at dusk, because you largely do.

Book for a Sunday-to-Tuesday stay to avoid the weekend wedding schedule. Order the Los Hermanos tasting on arrival and work through the Nebbiolo at dinner.
